workspace.heading: My Vault
workspace.empty: No entries yet. Add your first access.
workspace.staleNotice: Another browser has updated the vault. The latest version has been loaded. Please save again.
workspace.editModeNotice: Edit mode active. Do not enter password values.

entryForm.passwordHintPlaceholder: Example: In password manager 1Password under the provider name. Unlock via Touch ID on Max's MacBook.
entryForm.passwordHintHelp: Clearly describe <strong_where>where</strong_where> the password is stored so your next of kin can find it easily. Also mention any additional devices required (e.g. smartphone, Touch ID on Mac "XYZ", hardware key). <strong_nopassword>Never enter the password itself here.</strong_nopassword> Store passwords exclusively in a professional password manager and only reference it here.

settings.heading: Vault settings
settings.lead: Export your entries as a password-protected ZIP file (AES-256) for a secure backup.
settings.noticeDefault: Set a password to create the encrypted ZIP.
settings.noticeBlocked: Please set a password with at least 8 characters.

deleteAccount.heading: Permanently delete account
deleteAccount.lead: This permanently and irreversibly deletes your account and all related data.
deleteAccount.exportFirst: Please export your entries (above) before deleting your account.
deleteAccount.legal1: If you continue, the following is permanently and irreversibly deleted:
deleteAccount.legalVaults: Your entire vault including all entries.
deleteAccount.legalShares: All shares — invited trusted people are notified by email.
deleteAccount.legalNoRestore: There is no recovery. Nothing can be brought back.
deleteAccount.ackLabel: I understand that this action is final and nothing can be restored.

managePasskeys.heading: Passkeys
managePasskeys.hint: Every device you add can unlock your vault. The last passkey cannot be removed.

errors.prfNotSupported: Passkey does not support PRF. Please use a PRF-capable device.
errors.prfKeyNotFound: No key stored for this passkey.

wizard.step1Subtitle: Your passkey secures this vault. The key never leaves your browser.
wizard.step2Subtitle: Your only fallback. NachKlang cannot resend it.
wizard.step3Subtitle: Enter the recovery code again to confirm.
wizard.passkeyErrorGeneric: Could not create passkey. Please use a PRF-capable device.

passkey.hintCapable: Your vault will be secured with a passkey on this device. Nothing leaves your browser unencrypted.
passkey.hintIncapable: This device may not support PRF-capable passkeys. Please use a current device with a platform passkey.

recovery.printNote: Keep this code in a safe place. It is your only way to restore your vault if your passkey is no longer available. NachKlang cannot resend the code.

recoveryPrint.heading: NachKlang Recovery Kit
recoveryPrint.note: Keep this code in a safe place. It is your only way to restore your vault if you forget the passphrase. NachKlang cannot resend the code.

confirmation.consentLabel: I have stored the code safely and understand it is my only fallback.

audit.heading: Activity log
audit.lead: A traceable overview of all activity on your account and vault — including deletions by system administration or automation.
audit.empty: There are no log entries yet.

event.shareSealed: You granted a trusted person read access.
event.shareRevoked: You revoked a trusted person's access.
event.sharePurged: You permanently deleted a trusted person.
event.itemDeleted: An entry was deleted from your vault.

hero.lede: A vault for online accounts that never sees your passwords — reaching exactly the people you want to hand the key to one day.

links.donateTip: Want to keep the platform alive? Feel free to donate towards secure hosting on Hetzner.

cookieBanner.title: Cookies & Privacy
cookieBanner.text: NachKlang uses only technically necessary cookies for authentication and session management. No tracking, no advertising. Learn more in our <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink>.

info.labelResponsible: Responsible for content under § 18 para. 2 MStV

impressum.title: Legal Notice
impressum.intro: Information pursuant to § 5 Telemedia Act (TMG) and § 18 para. 2 Interstate Media Treaty (MStV).
impressum.sectionResponsibleTitle: Responsible for content under § 18 para. 2 MStV
impressum.sectionDisputeBody: The European Commission provides a platform for online dispute resolution: <odrLink>ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr</odrLink>. We are neither willing nor obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.
impressum.sectionLiabilityContentBody: As a service provider, we are responsible for our own content on these pages in accordance with § 7 para. 1 TMG. However, pursuant to §§ 8 to 10 TMG, we are not obliged to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information or to investigate circumstances indicating illegal activity.
impressum.sectionLiabilityLinksBody: Our website contains links to external third-party websites over whose content we have no control. The respective provider or operator of the linked pages is always responsible for their content.

datenschutz.title: Privacy Policy
datenschutz.introTemplate: Effective: {date}. This policy informs you pursuant to Art. 13 GDPR about the personal data we process when you use {product}, for what purpose, and on what legal basis.
datenschutz.s1Body: The controller within the meaning of the GDPR is:
datenschutz.s2Body: {product} is built as a zero‑knowledge vault. Your vault contents (credentials, notes, metadata about online services) are encrypted and decrypted exclusively in your browser. On our servers these contents exist only in encrypted form (AES‑256‑GCM; older entries may be encrypted with XChaCha20‑Poly1305 and are migrated automatically). The key never leaves your device — not even in our servers' memory. We technically cannot view, recover, or decrypt the contents of your vault.
datenschutz.s31Body: When you register, we process your name and email address. Sign‑in is passwordless via passkey (WebAuthn). To verify your identity at registration and as a fallback sign‑in, we send one‑time codes (OTP) by email. We do not store any passwords. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR (performance of a contract).
datenschutz.s32Body: For passwordless sign‑in we store your passkeys' credentials (credential ID and public key). The private key remains solely on your device and is never transmitted to us. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in account security).
datenschutz.s33Body: All vault contents are stored exclusively in encrypted form. Server-side we store only ciphertext, nonce, and bound metadata (vault ID, item ID, revision counter). Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR.
datenschutz.s34Body: When accessing the application, our hosting provider processes technically necessary data (IP address, date and time, requested URL, user agent). This data is stored for a maximum of 14 days to prevent abuse. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR.
datenschutz.s35Body: To prevent automated attacks and mass registrations, we use Cloudflare Turnstile during registration. Your IP address and technical browser information are transmitted to and processed by Cloudflare to verify whether the request comes from a human. The provider is Cloudflare, Inc. (USA); a data processing agreement with standard contractual clauses is in place. Turnstile does not set tracking cookies. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in preventing abuse).
datenschutz.s4Body: {product} uses only technically necessary cookies required for authentication and session handling. No tracking, analytics, or advertising cookies are set. Legal basis: § 25 para. 2 no. 2 TTDSG.
datenschutz.s5Body: We host {product} with {provider}. A data processing agreement pursuant to Art. 28 GDPR is in place. Processing takes place exclusively on servers in Germany.
datenschutz.s6Body: Transactional emails (verification, security notifications) are sent via {mailService}. A data processing agreement is in place. Recipient address and delivery status are stored for a maximum of 30 days.
datenschutz.s7Intro: You have the right at any time to:
datenschutz.s7Right1: access your data stored with us (Art. 15 GDPR),
datenschutz.s7ContactPrefix: To exercise these rights, an informal message to our data protection contact is sufficient:
datenschutz.s8Body: You have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority. The authority responsible for us is the Hamburg Commissioner for Data Protection and Freedom of Information, Ludwig-Erhard-Strasse 22, 20459 Hamburg.
datenschutz.s9Body: We implement technical and organisational measures to protect your data against accidental or intentional manipulation, loss, destruction, or unauthorised access. These include TLS transport encryption, encrypted databases, regular security audits, and the zero-knowledge model.
datenschutz.s10Body: We reserve the right to update this privacy policy if required for legal or technical reasons. We will announce material changes by email.

copyright.title: Copyright & Trademarks
copyright.s1Body: The content, works, and designs published on the pages of {product} — in particular texts, graphics, word marks, logos, and the application source code — are subject to German copyright law and international copyright conventions. Any reproduction, editing, distribution, or other exploitation beyond the limits of copyright law requires the prior written consent of {company}.
copyright.s2Body: "NachKlang" and the associated word-figurative mark are trademarks of {company}. Use without express permission — especially for commercial purposes or in a manner likely to cause confusion — is not permitted. References to third-party trademarks serve identification purposes only and remain the property of their respective owners.
copyright.s3Body: {product} uses open-source components licensed under their respective licences (including MIT, Apache 2.0, ISC). A list of the libraries used and their licence texts is available on request.
copyright.s4Body: All word marks, icons, and illustrations were either created on behalf of {company} or sourced from royalty-free sources under a licence that expressly permits use in {product}.
copyright.s5Body: Please direct enquiries regarding licensing, reuse, or press material to <email/>.

otp.subject: NachKlang Email Code
otp.expiry: The code is valid for 10 minutes. If you didn't request it, you can ignore this email.
otp.footer: This message was sent automatically by NachKlang. Please do not reply.

invite.subject: {owner} wants to give you access to a digital legacy
invite.heading: You have been invited as a trustee
invite.intro: {owner} would like to give you read access to their digital legacy in NachKlang. Everything stays end-to-end encrypted — you only gain access after a shared confirmation step.
invite.expiry: This link is valid for {days} days. If you weren't expecting this invitation, you can ignore this email.
invite.footer: This message was sent automatically by NachKlang. Please do not reply.

offboarding.subject: {owner} deleted their NachKlang account — your access has ended
offboarding.intro: {owner} has permanently deleted their NachKlang account.
offboarding.body: Your read access to the shared digital legacy has therefore ended. All related data has been irreversibly removed.
offboarding.footer: This message was sent automatically by NachKlang. Please do not reply.

errors.tokenMissing: No access token included in the server response.

owner.heading: Trustees
owner.lead: Give a trusted person read access to your digital legacy. Access is end-to-end encrypted and only becomes active after a shared confirmation step.
owner.invitedNoMail: Invitation created. Sending the email failed — share the link manually.
owner.fingerprintHint: Compare this code with your trustee in person or by phone before you grant access.

accept.heading: Access as a trustee
accept.lead: {owner} would like to give you read access to their digital legacy.
accept.invalid: This invitation is invalid or could not be found.
accept.notAcceptable: This invitation has expired or has already been used.
accept.successLead: Read this confirmation code aloud to {owner} so your identity can be verified. Once {owner} confirms, you'll get access.
accept.authPrompt: To accept this invitation you need a NachKlang account with a passkey (e.g. Touch ID, a security key, or a phone passkey).
accept.waitingLead: Show {owner} this confirmation code. Once {owner} grants access, the vault appears under "Shared with me".
accept.reopenNote: Afterwards, open the invitation link from your email again to accept.

shared.listLead: These vaults have been shared with you as a trustee.
shared.empty: No vault is currently shared with you.
shared.unlockLead: Unlock with your passkey to view the shared entries.
shared.forbidden: You don't have active access to this vault.
shared.error: Could not load. Please try again.
shared.handlingStatusHint: Your note only — the owner's entry stays unchanged.

guide.metaDescription: Step-by-step guide: create your account, set up a passkey, fill your vault, share safely and delete your data.
guide.fallbackNotice: This guide is currently available in German and English. More translations are coming.

securitySpotlight.title: Security first. Everything else after.
securitySpotlight.lede: Before you dive in: see for yourself how your data is encrypted inside your browser — and why our server can never decrypt it.

security.metaDescription: Interactive visualization of our zero-knowledge security architecture.
security.title: How We Protect Your Data
security.subtitle: An interactive guide to the security layers behind your zero-knowledge vault.
security.assuranceBootstrap: At no point does our server receive your raw Master Key or biometrics. Keys are mathematically wrapped inside your browser's secure sandbox before transmission.
security.assuranceEntry: All structural bindings (vault, items, owner) are cryptographically signed on the client. Our server operates exclusively as a blind ledger.

bootstrap.step1: Key Generation: A strong master key (Golden Key) is created directly inside your browser. It never leaves your computer.
bootstrap.step2: Fingerprint Lock: Your biometric passkey (Touch ID, Windows Hello) creates a unique digital padlock to protect your master key.
bootstrap.step3: Emergency Key: A long recovery code is generated and heavily processed to establish a second security padlock.
bootstrap.step4: Sealing: The master key is packed into two independent, encrypted security boxes (one for your passkey, one for your recovery code).
bootstrap.step5: Transmission: Only the sealed boxes are sent to the server. The original plaintext keys are wiped from browser memory immediately.

entry.step1: Validation: The browser checks your entry and forbids real passwords. Only location hints (e.g., 'stored in 1Password') are allowed.
entry.step2: Tagging: A permanent digital seal is attached to the entry, binding it irreversibly to your vault, account, and revision count.
entry.step3: Local Encryption: The entry is encrypted in the browser using the master key (AES-256-GCM). The plaintext remains safe on your device.
entry.step4: Storage: The encrypted envelope is sent to the server. The server only sees unreadable ciphertext and can never decrypt it.

bootstrap.step1: Root Key Generation: The client generates a 256-bit AES-GCM Root Key via the WebCrypto API. Once imported, it is isolated in RAM as a non-extractable CryptoKey.
bootstrap.step2: WebAuthn PRF Extension: Using the passkey's PRF extension, a biometric ceremony outputs a deterministic entropy value, stretched into a 256-bit KEK via HKDF-SHA-256.
bootstrap.step3: Argon2id KDF: The 120-bit Crockford-Base32 recovery code is stretched using Argon2id (64 MiB RAM, 3 iterations) to derive an independent recovery KEK.
bootstrap.step4: Root Key Wrapping: The Root Key is wrapped twice (once with the PRF-derived KEK, once with the recovery KEK) using AES-256-GCM with authenticated metadata (AAD).
bootstrap.step5: Zeroing & Sync: All transient plaintext key bytes are cleared via memzero. Only the wrapped key envelopes and KDF policy are synced to the DB via HTTPS.

entry.step1: Schema Checking: Input is validated via assertNoSecretFields() and a Zod schema to reject password-like keys and prevent secret exposure.
entry.step2: AAD Binding: Metadata (vaultId, itemId, ownerId, revision) is bound as Additional Authenticated Data (AAD) to prevent replay or cross-vault tampering.
entry.step3: AES-256-GCM Encryption: The entry is encrypted client-side using the non-extractable Root Key, a fresh 12-byte random IV, and the AAD metadata.
entry.step4: Server Sync: The server receives only the JSON payload containing the ciphertext, IV, and AAD. Without keys, the server cannot access the plaintext.
